Name: Redis Multiple Vulnerabilities (Oct 2025, RediShell)
description: Redis is prone to multiple vulnerabilities.   Quality of Detection (QoD): 30%
detection_method: Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.
insight: The following aws exist:
- CVE-2025-46817: Lua library commands may lead to integer overow and potential remote code execution (RCE)
- CVE-2025-46818: Running Lua function as a dierent user
- CVE-2025-46819: Out of bound read due to a bug in LUA
- CVE-2025-49844: Lua Use-After-Free may lead to RCE and dubbed 'RediShell'

Name: Redis RCE Vulnerability (GHSA-39h2-x6c4-6w4c)
description: Redis is prone to a remote code execution (RCE) vulnerability.
detection_method: Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.
impact: Redis prior to version 6.2.17, 7.x prior to 7.2.7 and 7.4.x prior to 7.4.2.
insight: An authenticated user may use a specially crafted Lua script to manipulate the garbage collector and potentially lead to remote code execution.

Name: Redis 2.6 < 6.2.16, 7.0.0. < 7.2.6, 7.4.0 RCE vulnerability
description: Redis is prone to a remote code execution (RCE) vulnerability.
detection_method: Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.
insight: Lua library commands may lead to stack overow and potential remote code execution vulnerability

Name: Redis < 6.0.20, 6.2.x < 6.2.13, 7.x < 7.0.12 Heap Overow Vulnerability
description: Redis is prone to a heap overow vulnerability.
detection_method: Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.
insight: A specially crafted Lua script executing in Redis can trigger a heap overow in the cjson and cmsgpack libraries, and result in heap corruption and potentially remote code execution.

Name: Redis DoS Vulnerability (GHSA-r67f-p999-2g)
description: Redis is prone to a denial of service (DoS) vulnerability.
detection_method: Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.
insight: An unauthenticated client can cause unlimited growth of output buers, until the server runs out of memory or is killed.

Name: Redis < 6.2.7 Multiple Vulnerabilities
description: Redis is prone to multiple vulnerabilities.
detection_method: Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.
insight: The following vulnerabilities exist:
- CVE-2022-24735: Lua scripts can be manipulated to overcome ACL rules
- CVE-2022-24736: A malformed Lua script can crash Redis

Name: Redis Buffer Overflow Vulnerability (GHSA-rp2m-q4j6-gr43)
description: Redis is prone to a buffer overflow vulnerability.
detection_method: Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.
insight: An authenticated user may use a specially crafted string to trigger a stack/heap out of bounds write on hyperloglog operations, potentially leading to remote code execution.

Name: Redis DoS Vulnerability (GHSA-4q32-c38c-pwgq)
description: Redis is prone to a denial of service (DoS) vulnerability.
detection_method: Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.
insight: An unauthenticated connection can cause repeated IP protocol errors, leading to client starvation and, ultimately, a denial of service (DoS).

Name: Redis < 6.0.3 Integer Overflow Vulnerability
description: Redis is prone to an integer overflow vulnerability.
detection_method: Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.
impact: Successful exploitation would allow an attacker to bypass sandbox restrictions or crash the application.
insight: The vulnerability exists in the getnum function in lua_struct.c.

Name: Redis Server No Password (TCP)
description: The remote Redis server is not protected with a password.
detection_result: Redis Server is not protected with a password.
detection_method: Evaluates if the remote Redis server is protected by a password.
- No scan result is expected if localhost (127.0.0.1) was scanned (self scanning)

- If the scanned network is e.g. a private LAN / private WAN which contains systems not accessible to the public (access restricted) and it is accepted that the service is accessible without authentication please set the 'Network type' configuration of the following VT to e.g. 'Private LAN' or 'Private WAN':
impact: This issue may be exploited by a remote attacker to gain access to sensitive information or modify system configuration.
insight: It was possible to login without a password.

Name: Redis 2.2.5 < 6.2.16, 7.0.0 < 7.2.6, 7.4.0 DoS vulnerability
description: Redis is prone to a denial of service (DoS) vulnerability.
detection_method: Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.
insight: Potential Denial-of-service due to unbounded pattern matching

Name: Redis < 6.0.18, 6.2.x < 6.2.11, 7.0.x < 7.0.9 Multiple Vulnerabilities
description: Redis is prone to multiple vulnerabilities.
detection_method: Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.
impact: Redis versions prior to 6.0.18, 6.2.x prior to 6.2.11 and 7.0.x prior to 7.0.9.
insight: The following aws exist:
- CVE-2022-36021: String matching commands (like SCAN or KEYS) with a specially crafted pattern to trigger a denial-of-service attack on Redis, causing it to hang and consume 100% CPU time.
- CVE-2023-25155: Specially crafted SRANDMEMBER, ZRANDMEMBER, and HRAND- FIELD commands can trigger an integer overow, resulting in a runtime assertion and ter- mination of the Redis server process.

Name: Redis < 6.0.19, 6.2.x < 6.2.12, 7.0.x < 7.0.11 DoS Vulnerability
description: Redis is prone to a denial of service (DoS) vulnerability.
detection_method: Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.
solution: Redis versions prior to 6.0.19, 6.2.x prior to 6.2.12 and 7.0.x prior to 7.0.11.
insight: Authenticated users can use the HINCRBYFLOAT command to create an invalid hash eld that may later crash Redis on access.

Name: Redis DoS Vulnerability (GHSA-2r7g-8hpc-rpq9)
description: Redis is prone to a denial of service (DoS) vulnerability.
detection_method: Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.
impact: Allows memory consumption via a multi-bulk command composed of many bulks, sent by an authenticated user. This occurs because the server allocates memory for the command arguments of every bulk, even when the command is skipped because of insucient permissions.
solution: No known solution is available as of 25th July, 2025. Information regarding this issue will be updated once solution details are available.
insight: Aected Software/OS Redis versions through 8.0.3.

Name: Redis < 6.0.17 DoS Vulnerability
description: Redis is prone to a denial of service (DoS) vulnerability.
detection_method: Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.
impact: Integer overow in the Redis SETRANGE and SORT/SORT_RO commands can drive Redis to OOM panic.
insight: Aected Software/OS Redis versions prior to 6.0.17.

Name: TCP Timestamps Information Disclosure
description: The remote host implements TCP timestamps and therefore allows to compute the uptime.
detection_result: It was detected that the host implements RFC1323/RFC7323.
The following timestamps were retrieved with a delay of 1 seconds in-between: Packet 1: 1907604711 Packet 2: 1907605819
impact: A side eect of this feature is that the uptime of the remote host can sometimes be computed.
solution: To disable TCP timestamps on linux add the line 'net.ipv4.tcp_timestamps = 0' to /etc/sysctl.conf. Execute 'sysctl -p' to apply the settings at runtime.

To disable TCP timestamps on Windows execute 'netsh int tcp set global timestamps=disabled' Starting with Windows Server 2008 and Vista, the timestamp can not be completely disabled. The default behavior of the TCP/IP stack on this Systems is to not use the Timestamp options when initiating TCP connections, but use them if the TCP peer that is initiating communication includes them in their synchronize (SYN) segment.

Name: TCP Timestamps Information Disclosure
description: The remote host implements TCP timestamps and therefore allows to compute the uptime.
detection_result: It was detected that the host implements RFC1323/RFC7323.
The following timestamps were retrieved with a delay of 1 seconds in-between:
detection_method: Special IP packets are forged and sent with a little delay in between to the target IP. The responses are searched for a timestamps. If found, the timestamps are reported.
impact: A side eect of this feature is that the uptime of the remote host can sometimes be computed.
solution: To disable TCP timestamps on linux add the line 'net.ipv4.tcp_timestamps = 0' to /etc/sysctl.conf. Execute 'sysctl -p' to apply the settings at runtime.
To disable TCP timestamps on Windows execute 'netsh int tcp set global timestamps=disabled'
Starting with Windows Server 2008 and Vista, the timestamp can not be completely disabled.

The default behavior of the TCP/IP stack on this Systems is to not use the Timestamp options when initiating TCP connections, but use them if the TCP peer that is initiating communication includes them in their synchronize (SYN) segment.   See the references for more information.   Aected Software/OS   TCP implementations that implement RFC1323/RFC7323.   Vulnerability Insight   The remote host implements TCP timestamps, as dened by RFC1323/RFC7323.  Vulnerability Detection Method   Special IP packets are forged and sent with a little delay in between to the target IP. The  responses are searched for a timestamps. If found, the timestamps are reported. Quality of Detection (QoD): 30%   Vulnerability Detection Result   Installed version: 5.0.14   Fixed version: 6.2.14   Solution:   Solution type: VendorFix   Update to version 6.2.14, 7.0.14, 7.2.2 or later.   It is also possible to work around the problem by disabling Unix sockets, starting Redis with a  restrictive umask, or storing the Unix socket le in a protected directory.  . . . continues on next page . . .

Name: Redis Unix Socket Permission Access Bypass Vulnerability (GHSA-ghmp-889m-7cvx)
description: Redis is prone to a vulnerability that allows to bypass desired Unix socket permissions on startup.
detection_method: Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.
solution: It is also possible to work around the problem by disabling Unix sockets, starting Redis with a restrictive umask, or storing the Unix socket le in a protected directory.
insight: On startup, Redis begins listening on a Unix socket before adjusting its permissions to the user-provided conguration. If a permissive umask(2) is used, this creates a race condition that enables, during a short period of time, another process to establish an otherwise unauthorized connection.

Name: Redis <= 7.0.5 DoS Vulnerability
description: Redis is prone to a denial of service (DoS) vulnerability.
detection_method: Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.
insight: A vulnerability, which was classied as problematic, was found in Redis. Aected is the function sigsegvHandler of the le debug.c of the component Crash Report. The manipulation leads to denial of service. The real existence of this vulnerability is still doubted at the moment. The name of the patch is 0bf90d944313919eb8e63d3588bf63a367f020a3. It is recommended to apply a patch to x this issue. VDB-211962 is the identier assigned to this vulnerability.
NOTE: The vendor claims that this is not a DoS because it applies to the crash logging mechanism which is triggered after a crash has occurred.

Name: ICMP Timestamp Reply Information Disclosure
description: The remote host responded to an ICMP timestamp request.
detection_result: The following response / ICMP packet has been received:

Name: ICMP Timestamp Reply Information Disclosure
description: The remote host responded to an ICMP timestamp request.
Quality of Detection (QoD): 80%
detection_result: The following response / ICMP packet has been received:
detection_method: Sends an ICMP Timestamp (Type 13) request and checks if a Timestamp Reply (Type 14) is received.
impact: This information could theoretically be used to exploit weak time-based random number generators in other services.
solution: - Disable the support for ICMP timestamp on the remote host completely
- Protect the remote host by a rewall, and block ICMP packets passing through the rewall in either direction (either completely or only for untrusted networks)
insight: The Timestamp Reply is an ICMP message which replies to a Timestamp message. It consists of the originating timestamp sent by the sender of the Timestamp as well as a receive timestamp and a transmit timestamp.

Name: CPE Inventory
description: This routine uses information collected by other routines about CPE identities of operating systems, services and applications detected during the scan.
Note: Some CPEs for specic products might show up twice or more in the output. Background:
impact: This information could theoretically be used to exploit weak time-based random number generators in other services.
solution: - Disable the support for ICMP timestamp on the remote host completely
- Protect the remote host by a rewall, and block ICMP packets passing through the rewall in either direction (either completely or only for untrusted networks)
insight: The Timestamp Reply is an ICMP message which replies to a Timestamp message. It consists of the originating timestamp sent by the sender of the Timestamp as well as a receive timestamp and a transmit timestamp.

Name: Traceroute
description: Collect information about the network route and network distance between the scanner host and the target host.
detection_result: Network route from scanner (172.30.0.2) to target (172.31.1.33):
Network distance between scanner and target: 2

Name: Traceroute
description: Collect information about the network route and network distance between the scanner host and the target host.
detection_result: Network route from scanner (172.30.0.2) to target (172.31.1.33):
Network distance between scanner and target: 2
insight: For internal networks, the distances are usually small, often less than 4 hosts between scanner and target. For public targets the distance is greater and might be 10 hosts or more.
log_method: A combination of the protocols ICMP and TCP is used to determine the route. This method is applicable for IPv4 only and it is also known as 'traceroute'.

Name: IP Forwarding Enabled - Active Check
description: Checks if the remote host has IP forwarding enabled.
detection_result: It was possible to route a TCP packet through the target host and received an an ->swer which means IP forwarding is enabled.
log_method: Sends a crafted Local Link Layer (LLL) frame and checks the response.

Name: Hostname Determination Reporting
description: The script reports information on how the hostname of the target was determined.

Name: OS Detection Consolidation and Reporting
description: This script consolidates the OS information detected by several VTs and tries to nd the best matching OS.
Furthermore it reports all previously collected information leading to this best matching OS. It also reports possible additional information which might help to improve the OS detection.
If any of this information is wrong or could be improved please consider to report these to the referenced community forum.
detection_result: Concluded from ICMP based OS fingerprint
Setting key "Host/runs_unixoide" based on this information

Name: Redis Server Detection (TCP)
description: TCP based detection of Redis server.
Quality of Detection (QoD): 80%
detection_result: Redis Server is not protected with a password.

Name: Service Detection with 'GET' Request
description: This plugin performs service detection.
Quality of Detection (QoD): 80%
detection_result: A Redis server seems to be running on this port.
insight: This plugin is a complement of the plugin 'Services' (OID: 1.3.6.1.4.1.25623.1.0.10330). It sends a HTTP 'GET' request to the remaining unknown services and tries to identify them.
